skill.name: sqlmodel-crud
skill.description: Guides AI coding agents through SQLModel model design, async CRUD operations, joins, relationships, database setup, and Alembic migration patterns.
skill.summary: Build SQLModel models, async CRUD layers, relationships, queries, and migration-ready database setup.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command and blocker patterns, but review found they are markdown code fences, inline code terms, and normal SQLModel examples rather than executable skill behavior. The only real concerns are a documentation example that reads DATABASE_URL with a placeholder default and links to external documentation; no prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged lines are fenced Python examples, inline code terms, file names, and checklist text inside SKILL.md, not executable Ruby or shell backtick calls run by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-number review shows markdown fences and inline code markers around Python documentation examples. No executable skill script, shell invocation, or Ruby backtick expression was found.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Are Benign External URLs
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are reference links to SQLModel, SQLAlchemy, Pydantic, and Alembic documentation. They are not fetched automatically and do not receive user data or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in the References section and point to public project documentation. There is no network client code associated with them.

low_findings.title: Example Database Configuration Includes Env Access
low_findings.description: The database setup example reads DATABASE_URL and includes a placeholder PostgreSQL connection string with user:password plus echo=True. This is documentation-only, but generated production code should replace placeholders and avoid verbose SQL logging.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The env access and connection string are clearly inside a fenced database.py example. It is a legitimate SQLModel pattern, but it can become risky if copied unchanged into production.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The blocker findings for weak cryptographic algorithms do not correspond to cryptographic calls. Reviewed lines contain front matter, field descriptions, pagination ordering, and documentation headings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations contain no MD5, SHA1, DES, or cryptographic API usage. The hits appear to come from broad keyword matching in normal documentation text.

content.value_statement: SQLModel projects need consistent models, relationships, queries, and async CRUD code. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured patterns for database-backed FastAPI work.
actual_capabilities: Defines SQLModel table models with fields, validators, indexes, timestamps, and relationships.
Shows async CRUD methods using AsyncSession, select queries, commits, refreshes, and typed returns.
Provides examples for joins, filters, pagination, eager loading, aggregations, and search queries.
Documents one-to-many, many-to-many, one-to-one, and self-referential relationship patterns.
Includes database setup and Alembic migration guidance for SQLModel projects.
Frames output expectations for models, CRUD modules, query modules, and project documentation.
limitations: It is guidance text, not a runnable package or code generator by itself.
Examples use school ERP entities and may need adaptation for other domains.
It does not provide tests, migrations, or security hardening for every generated model.
Production credentials, logging, and access control must be reviewed separately.

use_cases.title: Create a Backend Data Layer
use_cases.description: Generate SQLModel tables, async CRUD methods, and relationship patterns for a new FastAPI backend.

use_cases.title: Standardize Database Patterns
use_cases.description: Apply consistent field validation, indexes, pagination, and typed returns across multiple database modules.

use_cases.title: Extend Existing ERP Models
use_cases.description: Add linked entities such as students, fees, attendance, classes, and parents with appropriate relationships.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Model
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one SQLModel table.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SQLModel table for a student profile with validated fields, indexes, timestamps, and a short explanation of each design choice.

prompt_templates.title: Add CRUD Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Build async CRUD methods for this SQLModel entity using AsyncSession. Include create, get by id, list with pagination, update, delete, and count.

prompt_templates.title: Model Relationships
prompt_templates.prompt: Design SQLModel relationships for students, classes, fees, and attendance. Include foreign keys, back_populates, and recommended indexes.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize Advanced Queries
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for production query patterns.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these SQLModel queries for joins, filters, eager loading, pagination, and aggregation performance. Suggest safer and faster patterns.

output_examples.input: Create a Student model with class, fee, and attendance relationships.
output_examples.output: A structured model plan with validated student fields, foreign keys, bidirectional relationships, indexes, and timestamp guidance.

output_examples.input: Add async CRUD for fees with pagination and filtering.
output_examples.output: A CRUD design covering creation, lookup, filtered listing, updates, deletion, transaction handling, and typed return behavior.

output_examples.input: Explain how to query students with pending fees.
output_examples.output: A query strategy using linked fee records, filters, pagination, ordering, and notes about eager loading and index selection.

best_practices: Define relationships and indexes before writing CRUD methods.
Use async sessions consistently when the surrounding FastAPI app is async.
Review generated database URLs, logging flags, and password handling before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder database credentials into deployed configuration.
Do not mix sync and async SQLAlchemy session patterns in the same CRUD layer.
Do not expose password hashes or sensitive fields in response schemas.

faq.question: Does this skill run database commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for SQLModel code, but it does not execute migrations or connect to a database.

faq.question: Can it be used with FastAPI?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples target SQLModel and async SQLAlchemy patterns commonly used in FastAPI applications.

faq.question: Does it support relationships?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers foreign keys, back_populates, many-to-many link models, one-to-one links, and self-referential relationships.

faq.question: Does it generate migration files?
faq.answer: It gives Alembic setup guidance, but users must generate and review actual migration files in their project.

faq.question: Is it safe for production code?
faq.answer: It can guide production code, but credentials, logging, access control, and response schemas need separate security review.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
